title: Coreopsis monticola
intro: Coreopsis monticola, also known as mountain tickseed, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Coreopsis monticola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coreopsis monticola is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
taxonomy: Coreopsis monticola is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is classified as a perennial species, characterized by its bright yellow flowers. Coreopsis monticola is often found growing in woodland edges and rocky outcrops. Its classification also places it within the Coreopsis genus, which contains approximately 80 species of flowering plants.
uses: Coreopsis monticola has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in North America. Its roots, stems, and leaves are said to possess medicinal properties, although the exact uses are not well-documented. Traditional healers have used it to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues. Some sources suggest that it may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Coreopsis monticola.
